title: The One Where Rachel Has A Baby

jokes.text: We're off to a great start, aren't we? I knew I'd get here fast, but this has to be a record!
jokes.setup: Ross celebrates getting to the hospital in record time
jokes.punchline: Immediate ironic undercut when others beat them there
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for immediate ironic reversal. Well-structured but predictable.

jokes.text: How the hell did you beat us here? We took a cab. Did you walk?
jokes.setup: Monica asks how they beat them to the hospital
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic assumption they must have walked to be so slow
jokes.explanation: Good economic punchline that plays off Monica's competitive nature.

jokes.text: Okay, is there some kind of magic tunnel to this hospital?
jokes.setup: Ross is confused about how everyone beat them there
jokes.punchline: Absurd magical explanation for the timing discrepancy
jokes.explanation: Escalates Ross's frustration into absurdist territory with good economy.

jokes.text: Man, if only you'd gotten here sooner.
jokes.setup: Hospital says private rooms are unavailable
jokes.punchline: Chandler sarcastically blames Ross's driving speed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler callback that turns Ross's earlier pride against him. Excellent character voice and economy.

jokes.text: They're saving them for important people! What if I was the president?
jokes.setup: Rachel claims they save rooms for important people
jokes.punchline: Hypothetical presidential scenario while in labor
jokes.explanation: Rachel's entitled logic escalating to absurd presidential comparison works but isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: We'd be in a lot of trouble. You don't know where any countries are.
jokes.setup: Rachel asks what if she was the president
jokes.punchline: Ross points out her geography incompetence
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific roast. Ross's deadpan delivery of Rachel's weakness is perfectly economical and mean in a loving way.

jokes.text: This is a hospital. / You know what? I don't really care for your tone.
jokes.setup: Ross tries to bribe nurse, nurse states obvious
jokes.punchline: Rachel escalates by criticizing the nurse's tone
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Rachel's entitled attitude even in labor.

jokes.text: Like to see a semi-private room? / Yeah, it couldn't hurt to look.
jokes.setup: Rachel threatens to leave hospital mid-contraction
jokes.punchline: Immediate capitulation when contraction hits
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy beats demanding attitude. Good timing of pain undermining pride.

jokes.text: Ross putting on hospital gown backwards, Rachel laughing
jokes.setup: Ross trying to help by putting on hospital gown
jokes.punchline: Visual of him wearing it backwards/wrong way
jokes.explanation: Standard visual gag. Ross's incompetence with simple tasks fits character but isn't original.

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Ross. I'm here to ruin this magical day for you.
jokes.setup: Ross introduces himself to the other couple
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of his tendency to create drama
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware introduction that perfectly captures Ross's neurotic self-knowledge. Great economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Let me give you guys some privacy. / Nonsense. We're all in this together.
jokes.setup: Ross tries to be polite and give them space
jokes.punchline: Marc insists on making it a group experience
jokes.explanation: Sets up the annoying oversharing couple dynamic effectively.

jokes.text: Hey, smile! / No. / I really don't want any-- / Oh, thank you.
jokes.setup: Marc wants to take photos of Rachel in labor
jokes.punchline: Rachel's resistance crumbling under social pressure
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing how politeness forces uncomfortable situations.

jokes.text: Oh, honey, I think I'm having one too!
jokes.setup: Rachel having a contraction
jokes.punchline: Julie thinks contractions are contagious or wants attention
jokes.explanation: Absurd competitive birthing moment. Good character comedy showing Julie's need to be included.

jokes.text: Ugh, the miracle of birth sure is a snooze-fest.
jokes.setup: Three hours of waiting during labor
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic take on birth being miraculous
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler voice contrasting societal expectations with boring reality.

jokes.text: I think we should try to have a baby. / Okay. / What's that now?
jokes.setup: Monica suggests having a baby
jokes.punchline: Chandler's voice goes high with shock
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Chandler's panic response, well-structured misdirection.

jokes.text: Oh, this is fun. You're ready to have a baby? My boy's all grown up!
jokes.setup: Chandler says he's ready for a baby
jokes.punchline: Monica treats him like a child who's maturing
jokes.explanation: Good Monica controlling behavior, treating Chandler patronizingly.

jokes.text: I was screwing with you to get your voice high and weird... like mine is now!
jokes.setup: Monica reveals she was testing Chandler
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment her voice is now high from excitement
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-referential humor where Monica acknowledges her own vocal reaction.

jokes.text: I just want Joey to be the father. / What? Are you crazy? / Right there! That's all I wanted!
jokes.setup: Monica says she wants a baby
jokes.punchline: Reveals she wants Joey as father to get Chandler's reaction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and misdirection showing Monica's manipulation tactics.

jokes.text: Julie's cervix is dilated 7 centimeters. That's about four fingers! Doctor let me feel it.
jokes.setup: Marc returns with medical update
jokes.punchline: Graphic oversharing about intimate medical details
jokes.explanation: Strong cringe comedy through medical oversharing. Character-appropriate but gross.

jokes.text: Well, you could feel Rachel's, then feel mine to compare. / We won't be doing that.
jokes.setup: Marc suggests Ross feel his wife's cervix
jokes.punchline: Offers his own for comparison, Ross's firm shutdown
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of inappropriate intimacy. Ross's deadpan shutdown is perfect.

jokes.text: No, Ross, Ross? Ross? My child has no father!
jokes.setup: Ross tries to leave Rachel with the weird couple
jokes.punchline: Rachel's dramatic abandonment claim
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Rachel's dramatic tendencies when abandoned.

jokes.text: Ma, you're asking me to marry you?
jokes.setup: Ross's mother wants to give him something
jokes.punchline: Ross misunderstands engagement ring offer as proposal
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with economical punchline. Ross's neurotic leap to wrong conclusion.

jokes.text: Honestly, this isn't just some girl you picked up in a bar and humped.
jokes.setup: Ross's mom arguing for marriage
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual reference from prim mother figure
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Judy's prim exterior contrasted with crude language.

jokes.text: If you don't, I'll talk more about humping.
jokes.setup: Judy threatens Ross if he doesn't consider marriage
jokes.punchline: Threatens more uncomfortable sexual talk
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the uncomfortable mother-son dynamic.

jokes.text: Right. And with who again?
jokes.setup: Ross says he has to go have a baby
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's pointed reminder about who he's having it with
jokes.explanation: Sharp Phoebe observation pointing out Ross's avoidance of the obvious.

jokes.text: She's like the perfect woman. I know she turned me down... but if she wanted to be with me, I would take her in my arms and...
jokes.setup: Joey praising Rachel
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate romantic fantasy about pregnant friend
jokes.explanation: Good Joey character moment showing his inappropriate timing and romantic delusions.

jokes.text: I haven't bummed you guys out like this in a while.
jokes.setup: Joey realizes he's being inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of his pattern of uncomfortable oversharing
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware humor where Joey acknowledges his own social awkwardness patterns.

jokes.text: What happened to the Disgustingtons?
jokes.setup: Ross asks about the previous couple
jokes.punchline: Creates insulting family name based on their behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay creating a fake aristocratic name. Very quotable and character-appropriate for Ross's wit.

jokes.text: Not before she gave me a nice, juicy shot of little Jamie, crowning away.
jokes.setup: Rachel explains what happened before the couple left
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of unwanted birth viewing
jokes.explanation: Good cringe payoff to the oversharing couple's inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: Including, um, 'Evil Bitch' and, uh, 'Sick Bastard.'
jokes.setup: Rachel says new couple has unusual pet names
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're actually insults, not affectionate nicknames
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection setup - 'pet names' creates expectation of affection, delivers hostility.

jokes.text: She's in labor. You like that, you sick son of a bitch?
jokes.setup: Woman accusing husband of looking at Rachel
jokes.punchline: Bizarre accusation that he's aroused by labor
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of jealousy into completely inappropriate territory.

jokes.text: See? It's because you were looking, you fat pervert.
jokes.setup: Rachel has another contraction
jokes.punchline: Woman blames her husband for causing Rachel's pain
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the woman's paranoid logic connecting unrelated events.

jokes.text: Wanna live to see your baby? / Don't you talk to my husband like that, you stupid bastard!
jokes.setup: Ross threatens the looking husband
jokes.punchline: Wife defends husband while calling Ross worse names
jokes.explanation: Ironic that she defends husband from insults while insulting others.

jokes.text: Oh, good God! If you want a baby so bad, just go steal it!
jokes.setup: Rachel frustrated with the fighting couple
jokes.punchline: Suggests extreme solution of baby theft
jokes.explanation: Rachel's exasperation leads to darkly absurd suggestion.

jokes.text: We talk about having babies someday, not now... but I'm starting to think that we can handle it. We're good. We're really good.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks why Monica wants a baby now
jokes.punchline: Monica's self-congratulatory reasoning based on their relationship
jokes.explanation: Good Monica character moment showing her competitive self-assessment.

jokes.text: It's not right! We're not ready for a kid! / What? / I'm kidding. This is gonna be fun.
jokes.setup: Monica says maybe they're ready
jokes.punchline: Chandler panics then reveals he's joking
jokes.explanation: Typical Chandler panic-then-joking pattern, but well-executed.

jokes.text: I'm not comfortable doing this in front of the babies.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler discussing having sex to get pregnant
jokes.punchline: Chandler's concern about baby voyeurs
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler neurosis about inappropriate audiences.

jokes.text: We got time to kill. We're in a building that's full of beds! And it's so clean!
jokes.setup: Monica suggesting they have sex in the hospital
jokes.punchline: Her excitement about the cleanliness factor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica character moment - her cleanliness obsession overriding appropriateness.

jokes.text: No. / I'll see you downstairs then. / All right. / Hey, I got one! I got one!
jokes.setup: Joey trying to get snacks from vending machine
jokes.punchline: Celebration over successfully getting a snack after apparent struggle
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy with vending machine. Joey's childlike excitement fits character.

jokes.text: I hate to be a ball-buster, but can I just do it?
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking to press elevator button
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment she's being demanding about minor thing
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe self-awareness about her quirky controlling behaviors.

jokes.text: Let's just say, there was a typographical error with a sex manual.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asked how she broke her leg
jokes.punchline: Bizarre explanation involving sex manual typo
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist Phoebe line. Original concept, perfect economy, highly quotable. Classic Phoebe weirdness.

jokes.text: Car accident. Idiot on a cell not paying attention? / Yeah. Me.
jokes.setup: Man asks if idiot caused her accident
jokes.punchline: Phoebe reveals she was the idiot
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect economy. Setup creates expectation of victim, delivers self-culpability.

jokes.text: Wait! What? No! Elevator! No! You gotta press the button.
jokes.setup: Phoebe flirting with guy in elevator
jokes.punchline: Panic as elevator doors close, needs help pressing button
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy payoff to her button-pressing quirk causing problems.

jokes.text: She doesn't need to be. She'll still have the baby before I do.
jokes.setup: Ross asks if new woman is pregnant yet
jokes.punchline: Rachel's exasperated certainty everyone will beat her
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Rachel's competitive frustration with birthing process.

jokes.text: Mm, mm. / [Long pause] / Oh, that was a big one.
jokes.setup: New woman claims to have contraction
jokes.punchline: Clearly fake/mild reaction followed by unconvincing claim
jokes.explanation: Good awkward beat showing obvious fakeness of her 'contraction.'

jokes.text: I know who you're talking about. / Yay! Great! What room is he in? / Sorry. That information is restricted.
jokes.setup: Nurse recognizes who Phoebe is looking for
jokes.punchline: Immediately shuts down with hospital policy
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline showing bureaucratic obstruction.

jokes.text: Dr. Drake Ramoray. It's Portuguese. / Damn it! We're losing precious time. You want this man's blood on your hands-- / Hands.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to impersonate doctor
jokes.punchline: Overdramatic medical emergency performance with correction
jokes.explanation: Good Joey character comedy using his acting background with Phoebe's corrections.

jokes.text: Oh, right! Ha, ha.
jokes.setup: Monica asks if they have a condom
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dawning realization they don't need protection to get pregnant
jokes.explanation: Simple realization beat showing Chandler's mental adjustment to trying for pregnancy.

jokes.text: He's not really my type. / Not you. Dr. Ramoray.
jokes.setup: Joey suggests Phoebe go talk to the guy
jokes.punchline: Phoebe misunderstands who should approach whom
jokes.explanation: Simple misunderstanding comedy between Phoebe and Joey.

jokes.text: Actually, it's more like this. / Oh, stupid metric system.
jokes.setup: Rachel thinks 3 centimeters is bigger than it is
jokes.punchline: Blames metric system instead of her own ignorance
jokes.explanation: Excellent Rachel logic deflecting personal ignorance onto external systems. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I know. I'm a neurologist. Dr. Wells is a woman. / That was a test. Good response.
jokes.setup: Joey's fake doctor story gets challenged
jokes.punchline: Joey's quick save claiming it was intentional test
jokes.explanation: Good Joey quick thinking to cover his mistake with fake authority.

jokes.text: I'm a doctor, not a mathematician.
jokes.setup: Asked to calculate age from birth date
jokes.punchline: Joey's ridiculous professional limitation excuse
jokes.explanation: Good Joey logic showing his inability to maintain the doctor act.

jokes.text: Do you sleep with women and never call them again? / No. / Excellent. Excellent.
jokes.setup: Joey asking inappropriate personal questions as 'doctor'
jokes.punchline: His obvious personal investment in the answers
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Joey's transparent agenda during fake medical exam.

jokes.text: Are you into any weird stuff, you know, sexually? / No! / Ooh. Wrong answer.
jokes.setup: Joey asks about sexual preferences
jokes.punchline: Reveals his disappointment with normal answer
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Joey expected weird preferences, implying Phoebe's taste in men.

jokes.text: Enter Pheebs.
jokes.setup: Joey finished interrogating the guy for Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Theatrical announcement like a play or movie
jokes.explanation: Joey's theatrical nature showing through with dramatic entrance announcement.

jokes.text: If you bring in one more woman who has her baby before me, I will sue you. Not this hospital. I'm gonna sue you.
jokes.setup: Rachel frustrated with other women having babies first
jokes.punchline: Threatens to personally sue the doctor, not the institution
jokes.explanation: Good Rachel escalation making personal threats over medical timing.

jokes.text: My husband is a lawyer. / Uh, Rach? / You get back on that case, honey!
jokes.setup: Rachel threatens lawsuit claiming Ross is lawyer
jokes.punchline: Maintains the fiction when corrected
jokes.explanation: Rachel's commitment to her lie even when Ross tries to correct her.

jokes.text: We're gonna be baby buddies!
jokes.setup: Janice appears as the new patient
jokes.punchline: Her enthusiastic announcement of shared experience
jokes.explanation: Classic Janice inappropriate enthusiasm. Character-perfect but predictable.

jokes.text: Squeeze your legs together, cover the baby's ears.
jokes.setup: Janice arrives as fellow patient
jokes.punchline: Rachel's desperate attempt to prevent baby from hearing Janice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation of Rachel's horror at Janice. Highly quotable and original.

jokes.text: I nabbed him at the dermatologist's. Thank God for adult acne!
jokes.setup: Introducing her husband Sid
jokes.punchline: Grateful for his skin problems that led to their meeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Janice oversharing with inappropriate gratitude for medical conditions.

jokes.text: You have to speak loudly, he's almost completely deaf. / Oh, there you go. / Of course he is.
jokes.setup: Janice explains her husband's condition
jokes.punchline: Ross and Rachel's resignation to another complication
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of complications with Janice's husband adding another issue.

jokes.text: Oh. Well, then shut me up. / Just tell me how.
jokes.setup: Janice realizes they're not getting married
jokes.punchline: Rachel's desperate plea for method to silence Janice
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline with Rachel's desperation creating perfect comedic timing.

jokes.text: Heh, heh, heh, heh, heh, heh, heh, heh!
jokes.setup: Janice having another contraction
jokes.punchline: Her signature laugh even during labor pain
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency - Janice's laugh even during contractions is character-perfect callback.
